2606.16843.title: Data-Driven Decoding of Russell's Circumplex Model of Affect
2606.16843.paper_summary: Affective computing increasingly relies on deep learning to represent emotions, yet latent spaces often remain opaque, high-dimensional black boxes. This paper investigates whether Transformers' embeddings recover the geometric regularities of Russell's circumplex model. We unify two complementary experiments testing the hypothesis that, after training models on text and speech, their resulting latent spaces encode a topology consistent with valence-arousal and reproduce human-like neighborhood relations. Specifically, we evaluate deep representations extracted from Transformer-based text (RoBERTa) and speech (wav2vec 2.0) encoders, along with a multimodal Transformer fusion architecture, across naturalistic datasets like MSP-Podcast and controlled LLM-generated stimuli. Our analysis reveals that multimodal fusion of text and audio yields perfect topological alignment with Russell's primary emotion ordering. Furthermore, in a zero-shot setting using generic text embeddings, projected fine-grained emotion terms fall close to their established human-mapped coordinates. Our contribution is a novel, data-driven framework for validating emotion models, demonstrating that Russell's circumplex structure is intrinsically encoded in the embeddings of these modalities rather than being solely an artifact of human labeling, thereby bridging the gap between psychological theory and representation learning.
2606.16843.paper_summary_zh: 情感計算越來越依賴深度學習來表達情緒，但潛在空間往往仍然是模糊的、高維的黑箱。本文探討Transformer的嵌入是否能夠恢復拉塞爾圓環模型的幾何規律。我們統一了兩個互補的實驗，測試假設，即在對文本和語音進行模型訓練後，其結果的潛在空間編碼了一個與價值-喚起一致的拓撲，並再現了類人鄰近關係。具體而言，我們評估了從基於Transformer的文本（RoBERTa）和語音（wav2vec 2.0）編碼器中提取的深度表示，以及一種多模態Transformer融合架構，在自然主義數據集如MSP-Podcast和受控的LLM生成刺激中進行評估。我們的分析顯示，文本和音頻的多模態融合與拉塞爾的主要情感排序實現了完美的拓撲對齊。此外，在使用通用文本嵌入的零樣本設置中，投影的細緻情感術語接近其既定的人類映射坐標。我們的貢獻是一個新穎的數據驅動框架，用於驗證情感模型，顯示拉塞爾的圓環結構在這些模態的嵌入中內在編碼，而不僅僅是人類標記的產物，從而縮小了心理學理論與表示學習之間的差距。
2606.16843.author: Amdjed Belaref et.al.
2606.16843.authors: Amdjed Belaref, Samir Sadok, Zineb Noumir, Renaud Seguier
